mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals strong labor market conditions, boosting corporate earnings and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust demand conditions, which correlate with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS/HOUST growth and SPX returns drops below 0.15 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) combined with rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS > 2%) signals a tightening monetary cycle, which pressures equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS > 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where INDPRO rises by more than 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST < 600k) signals weak demand for residential construction, which correlates with negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where HOUST falls below 600.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE < 5%) indicates a tight labor market, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS > 150 and UNRATE < 5% fails to produce positive 20-day returns in 50%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS > 360k) during periods of elevated unemployment (UNRATE > 7%) signals resilience in demand, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where RSAFS > 360k and UNRATE > 7%.
